nodes.label: Framework Independence
nodes.rationale: Ensure the SDK core remains 100% vanilla TypeScript. No React, Vue, Svelte, or other UI framework dependencies are permitted.

nodes.label: Strict Styling Isolation
nodes.rationale: Never apply styles directly inside ShadowMount that could bleed or pollute the host. All reset rules belong in SHADOW_RESET_CSS.

nodes.label: Pristine Flat String Exports
nodes.rationale: Any new wrapper classes, metadata attributes, or alignment markers must be filtered out in ShadowMount.extractHTML to ensure the consumer receives clean user markup.

nodes.label: rAF-Throttled Rendering
nodes.rationale: Canvas overlay drawings must never run synchronously; queue them on the next animation frame to prevent redraw bottlenecks.

nodes.label: Host-Delegated Transaction History
nodes.rationale: Delegates transaction history (Undo/Redo) to the host application to prevent desynchronization with host state like page metadata, code views, or other workspace widgets.

nodes.label: Zero Runtime Dependencies Principle
nodes.rationale: Ensures SDK is lightweight, framework-agnostic, and compiled to ESM with no external dependencies.

nodes.label: Browser-Native Performance Goal
nodes.rationale: Delegates CSS layout calculations to the browser's native C++ rendering engine to ensure Flexbox, Grid, wrapping and margins work natively.

nodes.label: Granular Synchronization Principle
nodes.rationale: Avoids virtual DOM reconciliation. Modifies structure exclusively through explicit mutation APIs to keep the SDK lightweight and predictable.

nodes.label: Flat String Bridge
nodes.rationale: Guarantees that exported HTML is completely clean, stripped of all SDK wrapper elements, metadata attributes, or interaction markers.

nodes.label: Wrapper-as-Frame Model
nodes.rationale: The canvas wrapper constrains the node's position/size, and the React component renders unconstrained inside it.

nodes.label: Operation-Driven State Synchronization Decision
nodes.rationale: Exposes discrete Operation delta payloads from visual gestures instead of managing a private, internal undo/redo history stack. Prevents state desynchronization in complex host environments with their own undo stacks.

nodes.label: Pluggable Inline Text Editing Decision
nodes.rationale: Restricts default inline text editor to plain-text modifications (contenteditable='plaintext-only') to avoid formatting inconsistencies and tag pollution. Exposes a pluggable callback for custom rich-text editors.

nodes.label: Native Class Manipulation Decision
nodes.rationale: Supports native element class name manipulation in addition to inline styling to support utility-class frameworks and design token systems. Prevents layout styling from polluting HTML with inline styles.

nodes.label: Granular Mutation Sync Boundary Decision
nodes.rationale: Defines sync boundary around granular mutation APIs to avoid performance overhead and complexity of DOM diffing and reconciliation inside the SDK.

nodes.label: Stylesheet & JS Execution Strategy Decision
nodes.rationale: Isolates styles via host-provided CSS injected into Shadow DOM scoped style blocks. Disables JavaScript execution inside the Shadow DOM by default to maintain visual integrity.

nodes.label: Remote Debugging
nodes.rationale: Server uses remote debugging to attach to running Chrome instances without starting fresh profiles, which prevents resetting active login sessions and other local user data.

nodes.label: Anti-Rationalization
nodes.author: ozy
nodes.rationale: Agents under pressure find loopholes and bypass guidelines. Explicit loopholes, red flags lists, and rationalization tables are required to force absolute compliance.

nodes.label: Claude Search Optimization (CSO)
nodes.author: ozy
nodes.rationale: LLMs use the description field for discovery. If the description summarizes the workflow instead of the triggers, the agent might summarize its understanding and bypass reading the full workflow.
